summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that a crafted HTTP request with path traversal sequences to the /files endpoint successfully accesses files from an unauthorized Artifactory repository, confirming the SSRF vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that a request to /files/../repo2-secret/secret.txt successfully retrieves content from the repo2-secret repository, which is outside the intended repo1 scope, proving SSRF via path traversal.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully built the marketplace binary, started a mock Artifactory server with two repositories (repo1 and repo2-secret), configured the marketplace to use repo1, and then demonstrated that a crafted request with path traversal accessed files from repo2-secret, confirming the SSRF vulnerability.
verification_method: The verify.sh script sets up a mock Artifactory server with two separate repositories (repo1 as legitimate, repo2-secret as unauthorized), starts the marketplace configured to use only repo1, then sends a crafted HTTP request with path traversal (/../repo2-secret/secret.txt) to access repo2-secret. Success is confirmed by detecting the secret file content from the unauthorized repository.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP request to /files/../repo2-secret/secret.txt successfully returned content from repo2-secret repository
The marketplace was configured to use only repo1, confirming unauthorized cross-repository access
Path traversal was not sanitized or validated in the Artifactory backend implementation
No authentication or authorization checks prevented access to the unauthorized repository

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh copies the target repository from the advisory session directory and mounts it into the container at /target-repo.
